title: Sauce Labs keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Sauce Labs access key detected in source code (UUID format). Sauce Labs access keys provide authentication to cloud-based automated testing infrastructure for web browsers and mobile devices. Exposing these keys allows unauthorized users to: execute automated tests consuming your test minutes and incurring costs, access test results and screenshots containing sensitive application data, run malicious code in your testing environment, access recorded videos and logs of test sessions, manipulate test configurations and capabilities, consume parallel testing slots causing resource exhaustion, and access tunnel connections to internal networks. Store access keys securely using environment variables (SAUCE_ACCESS_KEY, SAUCE_USERNAME) or secret management systems (AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, HashiCorp Vault). IMMEDIATELY revoke exposed keys from app.saucelabs.com account settings (User Settings → Access Keys) and rotate credentials. Enable IP whitelisting, use scoped access keys with limited permissions, implement usage monitoring and alerts, restrict access to specific data centers, and employ separate credentials per CI/CD pipeline or team.

Sauce Labs access key detected in source code (alphanumeric format). This key grants access to Sauce Labs cloud testing platform including automated browser testing, mobile app testing, and test result data. Exposed access keys enable attackers to: consume your paid test minutes and parallel testing slots, access test execution videos and screenshots (may contain sensitive application data), view test logs and debug information, execute arbitrary tests in your account, access Sauce Connect tunnel configurations (potential internal network exposure), manipulate test configurations and desired capabilities, and cause resource exhaustion through concurrent test abuse. CRITICAL: Immediately revoke this key from app.saucelabs.com (User Settings → Access Keys → Delete). Generate replacement keys with minimum required permissions. Implement secure storage using environment variables or secret managers (AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, Vault). Enable additional security measures: IP whitelisting for API access, scoped access keys per project/team, usage monitoring and budget alerts, data center restrictions, Sauce Connect tunnel authentication, separate credentials for CI/CD pipelines vs. local development, API rate limiting, and audit logging for all key usage.
